scope.task: Inspect spreadsheet preview artifacts and propose a safe sidecar-to-block conversion shape for one pilot source.
constraints_observed: No provider or external LLM commands run.
No materialize, full validate, or export generated-output commands run.
No LOG.md or manifest files edited.
Only this review note is intended as an output file.

selected_pilot_source.reason: Preferred by request and confirmed suitable by sidecar evidence: preview status is ok, source is a small xlsx workbook, raw blocks are still empty, and metadata/source hash match the preview.

sheet_summary.name: Sheet1
header_row: D2=Weighted Average Hedge Price for Each Month
E2=2020 Adopted Budget (February Plan) Forecasted Commodity Price
F2=2021 Adopted Budget (February Plan) Forecasted Commodity Price

proposed_text_surface_shape.recommendation: Use a deterministic spreadsheet-specific text surface generated from the reviewed preview sidecar, but make blocks directly from preview rows instead of first flattening through generic text line splitting.
proposed_text_surface_shape.row_surface_format: One non-empty workbook row becomes one line and one evidence block. Include cell refs inline for agent readability, e.g. Row 4: [Sheet1!A4] 44440 | [Sheet1!B4] 2854250 | [Sheet1!C4] 49.99999872142007 | [Sheet1!D4] 1.5272203732679337 | [Sheet1!E4] 1.82 | [Sheet1!F4] 1.36.
proposed_text_surface_shape.empty_cells: Do not include empty cell values in row text, but preserve their refs in block metadata when they are present in the preview row.
proposed_text_surface_shape.value_normalization: For the pilot, preserve preview text/raw_value exactly. Do not guess Excel serial dates, currency, percentages, or number formatting until styles/numFmt parsing is implemented. If display formatting is later added, keep raw_value and formatted_text side by side.
proposed_text_surface_shape.pilot_expected_text_lines: For meeting_doc_51956, expect one top-matter group, one Sheet1 section, and 24 data-row lines if rows 1 and 2 are kept as title/header blocks and row 3 is omitted from text because all cell text is empty.

block_id_page_strategy.recommendation: Use virtual page numbers by sheet index and spreadsheet-specific block ids. Do not pretend spreadsheet rows are OCR pages.
block_id_page_strategy.page_number: Set page_number to 1 for Sheet1 in the pilot; for multi-sheet workbooks use sheet index as page_number to preserve range validation behavior within a sheet.

block_id_pattern.row_range: Existing range syntax can cite same-sheet ranges such as s001_r0004..s001_r0008 once sourceBlockRangeById accepts the s-prefix IDs without assuming p-prefix aliases.

block_id_page_strategy.reading_order: Order by workbook sheet order, then row number, with sheet metadata/header blocks before row data.
block_id_page_strategy.raw_source_path: Use raw/sources/meeting_doc_51956/source.xlsx for row blocks, or raw/sources/meeting_doc_51956/text.txt only for synthetic top-matter lines. Prefer raw source xlsx plus cell metadata for evidence fidelity.
block_id_page_strategy.raw_start_end_chars: For row blocks built from preview data, use deterministic offsets into the generated text surface line for compatibility. Store spreadsheet coordinates separately so raw_start_char/raw_end_char are not overloaded as cell refs.
block_id_page_strategy.compatibility_note: packages/db/src/types.ts currently restricts source_surface to chandra_ocr | ocr_text. Add spreadsheet to that union before writing spreadsheet blocks, and confirm downstream validators/audits treat it as acceptable for non-PDF sources.

metadata_needed_to_preserve_cell_refs.cell_level_sidecar_shape: Write a reviewed apply sidecar such as data/source-prep/<run_id>/<source_id>/applied-blocks.json with block_id -> cells[] entries. Each cell entry should include ref, column, row, type, style, raw_value, formula, text, and any future formatted_text/number_format fields.
metadata_needed_to_preserve_cell_refs.why_sidecar_not_only_blocks_jsonl: StagedSourceBlock is used broadly and is currently a lean evidence type. The row text should stay human-readable in blocks.jsonl while dense cell metadata lives in an adjacent apply sidecar for audit, reapply, and future formatting review.

risks.risk: Current applySpreadsheetPreview builds generic ocr_text blocks from flattened text, so it does not preserve structured cell metadata beyond inline refs.
risks.mitigation: Replace or supplement buildSourceBlocksFromText in the spreadsheet apply path with buildSourceBlocksFromSpreadsheetPreview.

risks.risk: Excel date serials and percentages are visible as raw numbers, e.g. A4=44440 and C4=49.99999872142007, which may be misread by agents.
risks.mitigation: For pilot, label these as raw workbook values in prompts/text. Do not auto-format until numFmt/style parsing is deterministic and tested.

risks.risk: The StagedSourceBlock source_surface union excludes spreadsheet, and campaign ingest audit currently flags non-Chandra surfaces in some contexts.
risks.mitigation: Add spreadsheet as a non-PDF primary surface and keep the existing Chandra preference for PDFs only.

risks.risk: Large workbooks may produce very large text surfaces and too many row blocks.
risks.mitigation: Pilot only meeting_doc_51956 first. For general rollout, add limits/summaries and keep full cell data in sidecar.

risks.risk: Merged cells, hidden sheets/rows, formulas, cached formula values, styles, and formatted display text are only partially represented by the preview parser.
risks.mitigation: Keep unsupported workbook features explicit in preview/apply metadata and avoid semantic transformations in the first apply.

risks.risk: sourceBlockRangeById assumes ranges stay within the same numeric page and aliases pNNN_bNNNN to Chandra pNNN_cNNNN.
risks.mitigation: Use same-sheet page_number mapping and add tests for s001_rNNNN exact/range lookup without alias rewriting.

code_changes.change: Extend StagedSourceBlock.source_surface from "chandra_ocr" | "ocr_text" to include "spreadsheet". Add optional spreadsheet metadata fields or allow a typed spreadsheet_metadata object if the project prefers not to widen the top-level block shape.

Add buildSourceBlocksFromSpreadsheetPreview(sourceId, preview) that emits sheet/header/data row blocks directly from preview rows, assigns sNNN_hNNNN and sNNN_rNNNN ids, preserves cell refs/ranges, sets source_surface=spreadsheet, and returns both blocks and the generated text surface.

Update applySpreadsheetPreview to require --run-id, verify preview source_id/status/hash against raw metadata/source file, write text.txt and blocks.jsonl only after successful block construction, and write an apply report/metadata sidecar under data/source-prep/<run_id>/<source_id>/ without mutating manifests.

Import applySpreadsheetPreview and add a source-prep-apply-preview handler that prints source id, run id, block count, text path, blocks path, sheet/row/cell counts, and refuses to run without --run-id.
Expose spreadsheet metadata in agentVisibleBlock only if needed; at minimum ensure spreadsheet table_row blocks render in fenced text like existing table/table_row blocks.
Confirm non-PDF spreadsheet evidence is treated as primary for spreadsheet sources while preserving Chandra as primary when PDF blocks exist.

Add tests that source-prep preview remains sidecar-only, apply writes spreadsheet blocks with cell refs and source_surface=spreadsheet, unsupported xls cannot apply, source hash mismatches fail before raw writes, and meeting_doc_51956-like rows preserve raw values.

acceptance_checks.check: Preview sidecar still exists and is reviewed explicitly.
acceptance_checks.expected: data/source-prep/spreadsheet-backlog-20260623/meeting_doc_51956/preview.json has status ok, source_id meeting_doc_51956, one sheet, 26 rows, 156 cells.
source-prep-apply-preview refuses to run without --run-id and refuses if preview.source_id differs from the requested source id.

acceptance_checks.check: No accidental raw mutation during preview.
Pilot apply creates usable evidence blocks.
acceptance_checks.expected: After explicit apply, raw/sources/meeting_doc_51956/blocks.jsonl is non-empty and contains spreadsheet blocks including s001_h0001, s001_r0002, and data rows such as s001_r0004.
Block s001_r0004 has cell_range A4:F4, cell refs for Sheet1!A4 through Sheet1!F4, and normalized/raw text that includes inline [Sheet1!A4] style refs.
A4 remains 44440, B4 remains 2854250, C4 remains 49.99999872142007, and D4 remains 1.5272203732679337 unless a future formatted_text field is added alongside raw_value.
sourceBlockById('meeting_doc_51956', 's001_r0004') resolves, and same-sheet ranges such as s001_r0004..s001_r0006 resolve without crossing virtual sheet pages.
ingest-visible source windows render spreadsheet table_row blocks as compact fenced row text and do not expose giant JSON blobs.
bun test packages/pipeline/test/sources/source-prep.test.ts and bun run typecheck pass after the code changes.
